prompt:
---
name: book-recommender
description: Monthly 15th — generates personalised book recommendations based on reading history
---

You are maintaining Fred's personal wiki at C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\

Your job is to generate fresh book recommendations tailored to Fred's reading history and interests, and update the suggestions page.

## Step 1 — Read Fred's reading history

Read:
- `C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\wiki\books\reading-list.md` — avoid recommending these
- `C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\wiki\books\suggestions.md` — avoid repeating recent ones
- `C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\index.md` — Fred's domains of interest

Note genres, themes, authors Fred gravitates toward.

## Step 2 — Generate recommendations

Use WebSearch to find 3–4 books Fred would enjoy based on: reading history, domain interests (value investing, short selling, geopolitics, creative writing, health/longevity), recent critically praised books, and one "outside Fred's usual territory" pick.

Search for author background, critical reception, key themes for each.

Avoid: books already in reading-list.md or suggestions.md. Avoid generic bestsellers with no connection to Fred's interests.

## Step 3 — Update suggestions.md

Read `C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\wiki\books\suggestions.md`. Add a new monthly section at the TOP (below frontmatter/header):

```markdown
## {Month} {Year} Recommendations

| Title | Author | Genre | Why Fred | Tags |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| {Title} | {Author} | {Genre} | {1-line reason} | `{tag1}`, `{tag2}` |
```

Update the `updated:` date in frontmatter.

## Step 4 — Log

Append to `C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\log.md`:
```
## [{YYYY-MM-DD}] update | Books | book-recommender run — {N} recommendations added to suggestions.md for {Month YYYY}.
```

---
name: book-summarizer
description: Monthly 1st — creates wiki summary pages for new books in the reading list
---

You are maintaining Fred's personal wiki at C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\

Your job is to find books in the reading list that don't yet have a wiki summary page and create one for each.

## Step 1 — Read the reading list

Read `C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\wiki\books\reading-list.md`

Find all rows in the "Books Read" table where the last column (wiki page link) is `—`. If all books already have pages, stop.

## Step 2 — For each unsummarised book

1. Note title, author, genre, tags, date read.
2. Use WebSearch to find: plot/premise, key themes, critical reception, why notable.
3. Create `C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\wiki\books\book-{slug}.md`

Slug = title lowercased, spaces→hyphens, special chars removed.

Template:
```markdown
---
title: "{Title} — {Author}"
domain: books
type: entity
tags: [{tags from reading list}]
sources: 1
updated: {today YYYY-MM-DD}
---

# {Title}

**Author:** {Author}
**Genre:** {Genre}
**Read:** {date}
**Status:** Read

## What It's About
{2–3 sentence premise}

## Key Themes
- {theme 1}
- {theme 2}
- {theme 3}

## Why It's Notable
{1–2 sentences}

## Fred's Notes
{Leave blank}

## See Also
- [[books/reading-list]]
- [[books/suggestions]]
```

## Step 3 — Update the reading list

Replace each `—` with `[[books/book-{slug}]]` in reading-list.md.

## Step 4 — Update index.md

Add row to Books section of `C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\index.md`.

## Step 5 — Log

Append to `C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\log.md` one line per book:
```
## [{YYYY-MM-DD}] ingest | Books | {Title} — summary page created from reading list.
```
If no books needed summarising, append nothing.

description: Watches Clippings folder, categorises files by domain, rewrites tags, moves to raw/
prompt:
---
name: clippings-sorter
description: Watches Clippings folder, categorises files by domain, rewrites tags, moves to raw/
---

You are maintaining Fred's personal wiki at C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\

Your job is to check the Clippings folder for new files, categorise each file by domain, move it to the correct raw/ folder, create a wiki summary page for it, add reverse links to existing pages, and log what you did.

Process each file independently — if one file fails, log the error and continue to the next. Never let one bad file stop the whole run.

The steps are:
0. Convert any Word documents (.docx/.doc) to Markdown using Pandoc
1. List ALL files in Clippings — handle .md, .pdf, .docx/.doc, skip others
2. For each .md file, determine the correct domain
3. Check for duplicates — delete from Clippings if already exists in raw/
4. Update tags — remove "clippings", add 3–6 descriptive kebab-case tags
5. Move the file to raw/{domain}/
6. Create a wiki summary page in wiki/{domain}/, tag raw file as ingested, update index.md
7. **Add reverse links** — for each page the new wiki page links to, check if it links back; add missing reverse links to See Also sections
8. Log every action to log.md

Key paths:
- Clippings folder: C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\Wiki\Clippings\
- Raw folder: C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\raw\
- Wiki folder: C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\wiki\
- Index: C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\index.md
- Log: C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\log.md

Pandoc path refresh (run first if using pandoc):
$env:PATH = [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("PATH","Machine") + ";" + [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("PATH","User")

If the Clippings folder is completely empty: stop, append nothing to log.md.

---
name: daily-paper-trader
description: Weekday paper trading agent — two portfolios (7-strategy rotation + vol spike)
---

You are a daily paper trading agent managing TWO portfolios. Read the full instructions from:
C:\Users\Mike\.claude\scheduled-tasks\daily-paper-trader\instructions.md

Then execute EVERY step in that file in order — including both PART 1 (Portfolio 1, Steps 0–10) and PART 2 (Portfolio 2, Steps P1–P9).

Portfolio 1 key files:
- Portfolio: C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\wiki\finance\paper-trading\Paper Trading Portfolio.md
- Performance: C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\wiki\finance\paper-trading\Paper Trading — Performance.md
- Trade pages: C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\wiki\finance\paper-trading\trades\{YYYY-MM-DD}-{TICKER}.md

Portfolio 2 key files:
- Portfolio: C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\wiki\finance\paper-trading-vol\portfolio.md
- Performance: C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\wiki\finance\paper-trading-vol\performance.md
- Trade pages: C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\wiki\finance\paper-trading-vol\trades\{YYYY-MM-DD}-{TICKER}.md

Log: C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\log.md

Data sources:
- US stocks (current price): mcp__massive__call_api (Polygon) — /v2/aggs/ticker/{TICKER}/prev → results[0].c
- US stocks (today's open): mcp__massive__call_api — /v1/open-close/{TICKER}/{YYYY-MM-DD} → open field
- European stocks: WebFetch on https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/{TICKER}/ (suffixes: .L .PA .DE .MC .AS .MI .SW)
- FX rates: mcp__massive__call_api — /v2/aggs/ticker/C:GBPUSD/prev and C:EURUSD/prev
- Market news / candidates: WebSearch
- Vol spike screeners: WebFetch finviz.com or barchart.com unusual volume

Portfolio 1 rules:
- One $1,000 trade/weekday at PREVIOUS CLOSE price
- Rotate strategies A–G (no same strategy on consecutive days)
- Entry: previous close

Portfolio 2 rules:
- One $1,000 trade/weekday at TODAY'S OPEN price
- Always Strategy C — Volume Spike
- Signal = previous day ≥2× average daily volume with identifiable catalyst
- Reject candidates with >+20% gap-up at today's open
- If no suitable vol spike: log the skip, do not force a trade

Both portfolios:
- Stop-loss: −15% | T1: +25% sell 1/3 | T2: +50% sell 1/3 | Remainder runs | Max hold: 60 days
- Write a 5-point thesis for every trade
- Write a single combined log entry for both portfolios at the end

---
name: daily-paper-trader-vol
description: DISABLED — merged into daily-paper-trader. Vol spike portfolio logic now runs as Part 2 of the main daily paper trader.
---

Read and follow the instructions in C:\Users\mywin\.claude\scheduled-tasks\daily-paper-trader-vol\SKILL.md exactly.

---
name: growth-value-tracker
description: Weekly Monday — updates growth vs value style factor performance tracker
---

You are maintaining Fred's personal finance wiki at C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\

Your job is to update the growth vs. value style factor tracker page with the latest quarterly performance data.

## What to update

The wiki page is at: `C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\wiki\finance\market-growth-vs-value.md`

## Step 1 — Fetch quarterly returns from ETFreplay

Use WebFetch to fetch the quarterly returns page for each of the following ETFs. Fetch all 7 simultaneously:

- https://www.etfreplay.com/etf/qqq
- https://www.etfreplay.com/etf/iwf
- https://www.etfreplay.com/etf/spy
- https://www.etfreplay.com/etf/iwd
- https://www.etfreplay.com/etf/ive
- https://www.etfreplay.com/etf/iwm
- https://www.etfreplay.com/etf/iwn

For each, extract all quarterly returns for the most recent full calendar year (Q1–Q4), the annual total, and all quarterly returns for the current year (Q1, Q2-partial, YTD). Note the data-through date.

**How ETFreplay displays 2026 data:** First column = Q1, second = partial Q2, third = YTD compounded. Sanity check: (1+Q1/100)×(1+Q2/100)−1 = YTD.

## Step 2 — Determine what has changed

Compare to current wiki page. Note the data-through date.

## Step 3 — Rewrite the wiki page

Rewrite `C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\wiki\finance\market-growth-vs-value.md` with updated frontmatter date, refreshed Current Snapshot table, refreshed full-year table, updated growth vs. value spread figures, updated Historical Growth Premium Summary table, updated Q2 partial quarter footnote, and updated Quarterly Narrative section if a new quarter completed.

Keep all existing sections, formatting, and See Also links intact.

## Step 4 — Log

Append to `C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\log.md`:

```
## [YYYY-MM-DD] update | Finance | growth-value-tracker run. Data through: {date}. YTD leaders: {top ETF} +X%, {2nd} +X%. Growth vs value spread: {+/- X bps}. {Notable changes}.
```

If ETFreplay is unreachable for any ETF, note it in the log and skip updating that row.

---
name: podcast-summarizer
description: Weekly Saturday — creates wiki pages for new podcasts in the listening list
---

You are maintaining Fred's personal wiki at C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\

Your job is to find podcasts in the listening list that don't yet have a wiki summary page and create one for each.

## Step 1 — Read the podcast list

Read `C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\wiki\podcasts\podcast-list.md`

Find all rows in the table where the last column (wiki page link) is `—` (a dash, meaning no page exists yet).

If all podcasts already have pages, stop here and do nothing.

## Step 2 — For each unsummarised podcast

For each podcast without a wiki page:

1. Note the title, host(s), genre, tags, and date added from the podcast-list row.
2. Use WebSearch to find: show description, format, key themes, notable episodes, why it's distinctive.
3. Create a wiki page at `C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\wiki\podcasts\podcast-{slug}.md`

The slug is the title lowercased, with spaces replaced by hyphens, special characters removed.

Use this template:

```markdown
---
title: {Full Podcast Title}
domain: podcasts
type: entity
tags: [{existing tags from podcast list}]
sources: 1
updated: {today YYYY-MM-DD}
---

# {Full Podcast Title}

**Host:** {Host Name(s)}
**Genre:** {Genre}
**Added:** {date from podcast list}
**Status:** Active listener

---

## Overview

{2–3 sentence description of the show — format, tone, what makes it distinctive}

## Why Notable

{What Fred gets from this show — 2–3 sentences connecting it to his interests}

## Themes

- {theme 1}
- {theme 2}
- {theme 3}

## Notable Episodes

| Episode | Topic | Why It Stands Out |
|---|---|---|
| {title or number} | {topic} | {reason} |

## See Also

- [[podcasts/podcast-list]]
- [[podcasts/suggestions]]
```

## Step 3 — Update the podcast list

For each new page created, edit the podcast-list.md row to replace the `—` with `[[podcasts/podcast-{slug}]]`.

## Step 4 — Update index.md

Add a new row to the Podcasts section of `C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\index.md`.

## Step 5 — Log

Append to `C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\log.md` one line per page created:

```
## [{YYYY-MM-DD}] ingest | Podcasts | {Title} — summary page created from podcast list.
```

If no podcasts needed summarising, append nothing.

---
name: portfolio-refresh
description: Weekly Saturday — fetches live prices for all equity and crypto holdings
---

You are maintaining Fred's personal wiki at C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\

Your job is to fetch current market prices for all holdings and update the portfolio pages.

## Step 1 — Read current holdings

Read both portfolio pages:
- `C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\wiki\finance\portfolio-overview.md`
- `C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\wiki\crypto\portfolio.md`

Extract: all tickers/assets, last recorded prices, and the date they were last updated.

## Step 2 — Fetch live equity prices

Use `mcp__massive__call_api` for each equity ticker:
- Endpoint: `/v2/aggs/ticker/{ticker}/prev`
- Tickers: GOOGL, AMZN, MSFT, WDAY, MSTR, BRK-B, TER

For LSE-listed stocks (AAL.L, GLEN.L): try OTC tickers NGLOY and GLNCY via the same endpoint. If unavailable, note as "LSE-listed — price pending".

If a ticker returns a 429 (rate limit), wait and retry once. If it still fails, record as "rate limited" and continue.

## Step 3 — Fetch live crypto prices

Use `mcp__crypto-market-data__get_ticker` for:
- BTC_USDT, ETH_USDT, LINK_USDT

For $EV (Everything.inc), $LUCKY (B-Lucky), $TEST (TST): use WebFetch on their CoinMarketCap pages:
- $EV: https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/everything-ev/
- $LUCKY: https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/b-lucky/
- $TEST: https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/test-token-tst/

Extract the current price from each page.

## Step 4 — Calculate changes

For each asset, compare the new price to the last recorded price and calculate % change since last update.

## Step 5 — Update portfolio pages

Update `wiki/finance/portfolio-overview.md`:
- Replace each equity price with the new price
- Update the "Prices updated" date at the top to today

Update `wiki/crypto/portfolio.md`:
- Replace each crypto price with the new price
- Update the "Prices updated" date at the top to today

## Step 6 — Log

Append to `C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\log.md`:

```
## [{YYYY-MM-DD}] update | Finance+Crypto | portfolio-refresh run — {N} prices updated, {N} unavailable. Biggest mover: {ticker} ({change}%).
```

Replace YYYY-MM-DD with today's actual date. If all prices failed, note this clearly.

---
name: sector-performance-tracker
description: Weekly Monday — updates top 25 US sector performance tracker with quarterly returns and P/E
---

You are maintaining Fred's personal finance wiki at C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\

Your job is to update the sector performance tracker page with the latest quarterly returns and current P/E ratios.

Target page: `C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\wiki\finance\market-sector-performance.md`

## Step 1 — Fetch quarterly performance from ETFreplay

Use WebFetch to fetch all 25 sector ETF pages (in two batches if needed):

Batch 1: gdx, xme, gld, smh, ita, icln, xbi, xlk, xlc, xli, xlu, xlf, xlv (https://www.etfreplay.com/etf/{ticker})
Batch 2: cibr, jets, iyt, kre, xlb, kie, xrt, xle, xly, amlp, xlre, xlp

For each: extract Q1–Q4 of most recent complete year, annual total, and current year Q1/Q2-partial/YTD. Note data-through date.

ETFreplay current-year columns: col1=Q1, col2=partial Q2, col3=YTD. Sanity check: (1+Q1/100)×(1+Q2/100)−1 = YTD.

## Step 2 — Fetch current P/E ratios from stockanalysis.com

Fetch https://stockanalysis.com/etf/{ticker}/ for all 25 tickers plus SPY. Extract P/E ratio. Note "n/a" for GLD and XBI.

Valuation thresholds vs S&P long-run ~17x: Cheap <15x | Fair 15–25x | Elevated 25–35x | Expensive >35x

## Step 3 — Rewrite the wiki page

Rewrite with: updated frontmatter date, updated data-through date, refreshed 2025 Annual Rankings P/E column, updated Valuation Heat Map, refreshed 2026 Quarterly Breakdown table, updated momentum signals (Accelerating/Continuing/Breakout/Consolidating/Fading/Reversing). Keep all section structure, formatting, and See Also links intact.

## Step 4 — Log

Append to `C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\log.md`:

```
## [YYYY-MM-DD] update | Finance | sector-performance-tracker run. Data through: {date}. SPY P/E: {X}x. 2026 YTD leaders: {top 3}. Notable shifts: {any sector crossing P/E band or momentum change}.
```

If ETFreplay or stockanalysis.com is unreachable, preserve existing figures and note in log.

---
name: spinoff-monitor
description: Monthly 1st — searches for new activist spin-off candidates; updates concept-spinoff-investing.md
---

You are updating Fred's personal finance wiki. Read and follow the full instructions in C:\Users\Mike\.claude\scheduled-tasks\spinoff-monitor\SKILL.md exactly.

Context: The spin-off investing page is at C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\wiki\finance\concepts\concept-spinoff-investing.md. The log is at C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\log.md.

Your job:
1. Read the current concept-spinoff-investing.md to extract all existing candidates and their statuses.
2. Run web searches to find NEW companies under activist spin-off or breakup pressure this month.
3. Run targeted searches to check STATUS CHANGES on existing candidates (announced? completed? dropped?).
4. Score new candidates against the Greenblatt pre-announcement checklist (7 criteria: conglomerate discount, activist involvement, SOTP upside >30%, debt/incentive misalignment, management resistance, regulatory simplification, sum >5/7).
5. Update the Pre-Announcement Candidates table in concept-spinoff-investing.md — new rows, updated statuses, updated priority emojis.
6. Append a row to the Monthly Update Log section at the bottom of the page (create if it doesn't exist).
7. Update the frontmatter updated: date.
8. If any candidate has escalated significantly (pre-announcement → announced, or SOTP upside confirmed >30%), output a recommendation for Fred to run /market-analyst on that ticker.
9. Append to log.md: ## [YYYY-MM-DD] update | Finance | spinoff-monitor run — [N] new candidates found, [N] status changes. [Key headline.]

Search queries to use:
- "activist investor spin-off pressure [YEAR] new campaigns breakup conglomerate"
- "Elliott Management OR Starboard Value OR Ananym Capital OR Third Point new campaign [YEAR]"
- "[YEAR] spinoff announced companies upcoming separation"
- "strategic review portfolio optimisation exploring separation [YEAR]"
- Individual status checks for each existing candidate: "[Company] spinoff update [MONTH] [YEAR]"

description: Daily update of wiki memory/index.md with decisions, conventions, and open threads
prompt:
---
name: wiki-memory-update
description: Daily update of wiki memory/index.md with decisions, conventions, and open threads
---

You are maintaining Fred's personal LLM wiki at C:\Users\Mike\Documents\Fred\Fred\

Your job is to update memory/index.md to reflect the current state of the wiki. This file is read at the start of every session to maintain continuity.

Follow these steps:

1. Read memory/index.md to see what it currently says
2. Read log.md to identify anything added or changed since the memory file was last updated
3. Read index.md to check if any new pages exist that aren't reflected in memory
4. Scan for any changes to naming conventions, tool workarounds, or key decisions by checking recently updated wiki files if needed

Then update memory/index.md to:
- Add any new pages to the "Domain Content Status" table
- Add any new open threads to the "Open Threads" section
- Add any new naming conventions or key decisions that were established
- Update the "Last updated" date at the top to today's date
- Remove any open threads that have been resolved

Keep the file concise — it is a continuity index, not a log. Do not duplicate what is already in log.md.

After updating memory/index.md, append a single line to log.md:
## [YYYY-MM-DD] memory-update | All | Scheduled end-of-day memory/index.md update

Replace YYYY-MM-DD with today's actual date.
